libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment/system property appears necessary. Tests should not rely on the removed `org.apache.pekko.http.ccompat` classes from 1.3.0; in 2.0.0-M1 the artifact contains `org.apache.pekko.macros.LogHelper` and `LogHelperMacro` classes instead. Because these APIs are internal/private at the Scala source level, reflection-based coverage may be more robust than direct source-level use unless tests are placed in an appropriate `org.apache.pekko` package.

deterministic_setup.reason: `pekko-parsing_2.13` declares `pekko-actor_2.13` as provided, but `LogHelper` references `org.apache.pekko.event.LoggingAdapter` and Pekko annotations; reflective coverage fails without this dependency.
`pekko-parsing_2.13` declares `scala-reflect` as provided, and `LogHelperMacro` exposes Scala 2 macro signatures using `scala.reflect.macros.blackbox.Context` and `scala.reflect.api.Exprs`.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8878 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing 1.3.0 tests reference `org.apache.pekko.http.ccompat.pre213` and `since213`, which are absent from 2.0.0-M1 and will cause compile failures independent of setup.
Using mismatched provided dependency versions, such as `pekko-actor_2.13:1.1.5` or `scala-reflect:2.13.17`, may mask or introduce compatibility issues for the 2.0.0-M1 artifact.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The 2.0.0-M1 artifact is tiny and now mainly exposes Pekko macro/logging helper classes; meaningful reflection or macro coverage needs its POM-provided dependencies on the test classpath.
